reasoning_content:
Now I understand! The input to task1 is the preprocessed file from task0, which contains line markers. The line markers tell us the actual file location. I need to parse these line markers correctly to get the right file path and line numbers.

Looking at the preprocessed file:
```
# 1 "/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c"
# 1 "<built-in>" 1
# 1 "<built-in>" 3
# 389 "<built-in>" 3
# 1 "<command line>" 1
# 1 "<built-in>" 2
# 1 "/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c" 2
int main(){
    return 3;
}
```

The line markers have the format: `# linenum filename flags`

The last line marker before the actual code is:
`# 1 "/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c" 2`

This means:
- Line 1 in the preprocessed file corresponds to line 1 in the actual file
- The actual file is `/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c`
- Flag 2 means "entering a file"

So when I parse the preprocessed file, I need to:
1. Track the current file and line offset from line markers
2. When I see a line marker, update the current file and line offset
3. The line offset is the line number in the line marker minus 1 (since line markers are 1-indexed)

Let me fix the main.cpp to properly handle this.

tool_call.name: file_editor
